title: AMV command-response trace staging manifest (v1)
description: Metadata-only staging manifest for real AMV command-response actuation traces routed through the amv-calibration external-data path (issue #2415). Declares, per candidate trace bundle, provenance/license, the command and response channels the bundle would expose, the timing/latency fields a command-response calibration needs, and which canonical synthetic-actuation envelope fields the trace would calibrate. It describes staging intent only; it neither ingests traces nor stores raw command-response samples.

calibration_asset_id.description: External-data AssetSpec id the trace bundles stage through (the amv-calibration path in scripts/tools/manage_external_data.py).

synthetic_envelope_module.description: Path to the synthetic actuation-envelope module whose fields a calibrated command-response trace would inform (robot_sf/benchmark/synthetic_actuation.py).

asset_id.description: External-data AssetSpec id this trace bundle stages through, or null when no asset spec is wired yet. Defaults conceptually to calibration_asset_id.

redistribution.description: Raw external command-response traces are never redistributed by this repository.

command_channels.description: Commanded actuation channels the trace bundle would expose (e.g. linear_velocity_command_mps, yaw_rate_command_rad_s).

response_channels.description: Measured response channels the trace bundle would expose (e.g. measured_linear_velocity_mps, measured_yaw_rate_rad_s).

timing_fields.description: Timing/latency fields a command-response calibration needs (e.g. timestamp_s, control_latency_s, sample_rate_hz).

calibration_targets.description: Canonical synthetic-actuation envelope fields this trace would calibrate (validated against robot_sf.benchmark.synthetic_actuation.actuation_variability_fields()).
